name: Hasura GraphQL Engine
what_it_does: Hasura instantly generates a production-ready GraphQL (and REST) API from your existing PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, or other databases, with fine-grained row-level authorization, real-time subscriptions, and event triggers — without writing resolvers.
use_cases: Giving agents a GraphQL interface to query and mutate relational database data without a custom API layer
Using Hasura's permission system to enforce row-level security for multi-tenant agent applications
Setting up event triggers on database changes to kick off agent workflows
Exposing existing PostgreSQL databases to agents via auto-generated, strongly-typed GraphQL
Federating multiple data sources (Postgres + REST services) into a single GraphQL schema for agent consumption
Building real-time agent dashboards with GraphQL subscriptions over database tables
not_for: Teams that are not already using or willing to adopt GraphQL
Non-relational data patterns that don't fit well into table-based schemas
Agents that need heavy business logic in the API layer (Hasura delegates this to event triggers or custom resolvers)
Extremely high-throughput write workloads without careful query optimization
best_when: You have an existing PostgreSQL database and want to instantly expose it to agents via a well-structured, type-safe GraphQL API with built-in auth and row-level security, without writing a custom API layer.
avoid_when: Your team is REST-only, you have no existing relational database, or you need complex server-side business logic baked into the API layer.

auth.notes: Admin secret for privileged operations. JWT-based auth for user-scoped access with role claims embedded in the token. Supports webhook-based auth for custom session resolution. Row-level permissions defined by role in Hasura metadata.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted is completely free and open source. Hasura Cloud free tier: 60 requests/minute, 100MB storage, 1GB data passthrough/month
pricing.paid_tiers: Hasura Cloud Professional: ~$99/month baseline + usage; Enterprise: custom pricing with SSO, compliance, dedicated support
pricing.notes: Self-hosted EE features require a license key (some features are EE-only). Cloud free tier is limited but usable for development. Most serious production use cases use self-hosted or Cloud Professional.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no built-in rate limits (configure at reverse proxy). Cloud free: 60 req/min. Cloud Pro: configurable higher limits.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL errors follow the spec with extensions containing code and path fields. Query validation errors are precise. Permission-denied errors may reveal schema shape — worth considering for security.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Mutations that use insert_on_conflict with on_conflict clause support upsert semantics. Pure inserts are not idempotent. Updates are idempotent if same data.
known_agent_gotchas: Agents must learn Hasura's GraphQL query syntax including where clauses and _eq/_like operators
Large result sets require explicit limit/offset or cursor-based pagination — no automatic pagination
Subscription-based features require WebSocket connections — not usable in simple HTTP polling patterns
Schema changes require metadata reload — live schema changes can disrupt running agents
N+1 query problems possible if agents generate many small queries — use nested queries instead
Admin secret must be rotated carefully — it grants full database access
REST endpoints are custom-configured, not auto-generated — GraphQL is the primary interface

security_score_components.security_notes: Row-level security enforced via role-based permission rules in Hasura metadata. Admin secret should be treated as database root access. JWT validation is robust with configurable claim paths. Open source codebase is auditable.
